rulings.Case Title: M-J-SF INVESTMENTS LLC VS. DAISY MACARTHUR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Defts Motion To Enforce Terms Of Settlement Agreement And For Entry Of Judgment;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 18, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANTS GAIL HUYNH, LINDA D'SOUZA, BETTY WILSON Notice Of Hearing On Defendants Motion To Enforce Terms of Settlement Agreement and for Entry of Judgment. Off Calendar, Dismissal of complaint entered on 9/26/08 and it appears that the entire cross complaint has been dismissed. Court lacks jurisdiction to enter judgment in Defendants' favor under CCP 664-6. See Viejo Bancorp, Inc. vs. Wood, (1989) 217, Cal.App. 3rd 200, 206. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: DOUG SLAIN VS. PATH-WISE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice And Re Notice Of Motion For Leave To File An Amended Answer
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 18, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANTS PATH-WISE CORPORATION, ROBERT MULLENDORE, PRAKASH MUTTINENI (Amended) Notice and Re Notice of Motion for Leave to File an Amended Answer. Off Calendar. Opposition demonstrates that defendant is a corporation whose rights have been forfeited. Accordingly, Defendant cannot defend this action. The court would continue this matter to allow defendant to obtain a certificate of revivor and/or apply to regain its rights. See Color-Vue, Inc. vs.Abrams (1996) 44 Cal.App. 4th 1599. However, given the 2/22/10 trial date it cannot do so. Defendant to appear at 2/22/10 trial call and explain efforts to revive. =(302/AJR)

See Viejo Bancorp, Inc. vs. Wood, (1989) 217, Cal.App. 3rd 200, 206. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: BURNS TOMMY et al VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 18, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Continued to March 4, 2010 to be heard by Judge Woolard. Furthermore, plaintiff to provide courtesy copy of opposition with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: SHEILA TSANG VS. CONNIE CILETTI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 18, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT CONNIE CILETTI MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Is granted. Defendant shifted the burden that (1) Defendant is an original tenant, and (2) Defendant is a tenant whose occupancy Plaintiff consented to. Plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of fact by relying on defendant deposition tesimony or the Tsang declaration because those do not overcome Park Merced, 215 Cal.App.3d 490, and San Francisco Rent Rule 6.14, as those apply to the facts here. =(302/AJR)
